title: Authority

definition.short: Recognized standing to direct, decide, or govern within a governance order.
definition.full: Authority refers to recognized standing to make decisions, set direction, or govern within a governance order. It does not mean mere force, influence, or accepted validity.

coreMeaning: Who may direct within a governance order, not what can be made to happen or what counts as valid.

sourceReferences.label: Oxford Languages
sourceReferences.notes: Reference input only. Runtime output should use internal wording.

sourceReferences.label: John Rawls, A Theory of Justice

perspectives.summary: Authority should remain publicly justifiable through fairness.
